story: US-005-3: CommentThread Component — List and Add-Comment Form

testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. All tests passed without modification.

criteria.criterion: AC1: components/CommentThread.vue accepts projectSlug and ticketRef props
AC2: Comments are fetched via useAsyncData and rendered in chronological order
criteria.evidence: useAsyncData with $api.get() + v-for loop renders comments; API response is chronologically ordered

criteria.criterion: AC3: Each comment displays a colored type pill: VERIFICATION=blue, FIX_REPORT=orange, REVIEW=green, GENERAL=gray
criteria.evidence: Type mapping with Tailwind classes; rendered with dynamic class binding and rounded-full shape

criteria.criterion: AC4: Add-comment form has a required body Textarea and a type Select with all four options
criteria.evidence: FormField for body with Textarea; FormField for type with Select containing GENERAL, VERIFICATION, FIX_REPORT, REVIEW

criteria.criterion: AC5: Form POSTs to correct endpoint and appends comment reactively without full reload
AC6: Body field shows validation error if submitted empty
criteria.evidence: z.string().min(1, 'Comment body is required') + FormMessage displays validation errors

reasoning: All 36 tests pass. Implementation meets all acceptance criteria with clean, idiomatic Vue 3 + Nuxt code. Uses proper patterns: defineProps<>, useAsyncData, useApi, vee-validate + zod, Shadcn components. Error handling is comprehensive with try-catch and toast notifications. Code is 130 lines, well-structured with clear separation of comments list and form sections. No console.log statements. Proper TypeScript typing with Comment interface. No code quality issues detected.
